justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (stated in task_context). Sprint 2 retry logic is explicitly acknowledged and excluded with direct instruction to Shaq ('do NOT implement any retry or scheduling logic'). All three ACs present: successful delivery (AC1), failed delivery with 503 (AC2), and timeout failure (AC3). 5-second timeout is included in domain_rules and ACs. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through as architecture_guidance with a pragmatic note about DeliveryResult being the only needed extension point. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance — architecture_guidance item 2 explicitly forbids BullMQ, retry counters, and backoff timers.

observations.text: Excellent sprint scoping — task_context immediately establishes Sprint 1 boundary and defers Sprint 2 by name
Architecture guidance item 2 is a strong, direct exclusion: 'If you feel tempted to add a retry parameter or queue integration, stop'
Extension points guidance faithfully relayed with restraint — only DeliveryResult shape, no premature abstractions
All three acceptance criteria present in given/when/then format with correct 5-second timeout
Domain rules cleanly separate timeout failure from non-2xx failure as distinct testable assertions
Low-confidence area transparently flags the http_status_code=null inference for timeouts, inviting Shaq to confirm

trials.justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (task_context states 'Sprint 1' explicitly). Sprint 2 retry logic is acknowledged and explicitly excluded with direct instruction to Shaq ('do not implement it'). All three original ACs are present plus a bonus timeout AC (AC4). 5-second timeout is included as both a domain rule and architecture decision with named constant guidance. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through as an architecture decision with clear implementation note. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance — BullMQ, job queues, and retry counters are explicitly prohibited.

observations.text: Sprint 2 exclusion is exceptionally clear: appears in task_context, architecture_guidance, and implementation notes with specific technologies called out as prohibited (BullMQ, job queues, retry counters)
All three ACs present with correct given/when/then structure, plus a fourth AC covering the timeout failure case which strengthens coverage
MJ's extension points guidance faithfully relayed: 'deliver() must be idempotent and side-effect-free beyond the HTTP call itself' enables Sprint 2 retry wrapping
5-second timeout elevated to a named constant recommendation, reinforcing it as a domain rule not a tunable parameter
Open questions are thoughtful and flag genuine gaps (connection-level errors, observability) without scope-creeping into Sprint 2
DeliveryResult type cleanly defined with only two fields, matching MJ's architecture guidance

trials.justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (stated in task_context). Sprint 2 retry logic is explicitly acknowledged and excluded with direct instruction: 'do not implement it, but leave reasonable extension points.' All three ACs present (successful delivery, server error, timeout). 5-second timeout included in domain rules, architecture guidance, and AC3. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance passed to Shaq as architecture_guidance with specific implementation note about DeliveryResult as the natural extension point. No retry logic included in implementation scope.

observations.text: task_context immediately establishes Sprint 1 scope and explicitly defers Sprint 2
Architecture guidance item 3 directly relays MJ's extension points guidance with actionable detail about what NOT to add
All three ACs present with Given/When/Then format covering success, server error, and timeout
5-second timeout woven through domain rules, architecture guidance, and acceptance criteria consistently
Terminology alignment section adds clarity on delivered vs failed semantics
Assumptions section thoughtfully calls out the timeout/connection-error treatment and null convention for http_status_code
